raw_consumer_statement:
I am writing to inform you that XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX ( North Carolina XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ID # XXXX ) appears to be engaging in deceptive business practices regarding online sales to consumers in California. 

Today, I purchased a car part for {$230.00}. Within XXXX minute, I called the company to cancel, but they wanted to charge me a XXXX XXXX credit card refund processing fee. I explained to them that if you void the sale, there is no need for a refund and no credit card processing fees, aside from a possible {$0.00} authorization hold. 

I spoke to XXXX on the phone. He emailed me their terms and conditions, which were not made clear at checkout. Instead, the information was buried in a link that no reasonable consumer would click on. They are voiding the credit card charge and will not be charged the XXXX XXXX credit card processing fee ; therefore, by charging me XXXX XXXX, they are imposing a fee they will not incur. This fee was never properly disclosed or agreed to at the time of purchase, raising serious concerns about their transparency and fair business practices. 

I requested to speak to their owner, XXXX XXXX. Still, he refused to get on the phone, even after I explained to XXXX that I would report the matter to the California Attorney General and dispute it with American Express. XXXX stood firm and stated that XXXX said they would be charging the XXXX XXXX  credit card processing fee.They wound up refunding me {$220.00}, which is {$8.00} less than the refund should have been. 

I highly recommend that your office review XXXX XXXX XXXX of North Carolina and, if they are found to be out of compliance with their credit card processing practices in California, that they be fined or prevented from doing further business in the State of California.
